events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to judge the operator's reasoning against the specification. The scenario: operator reinstalled single-use stretch bolts (torque-to-yield) that were removed, reasoning they were only torqued once and replacement would stop the line. Engineering spec marks them single-use with angle-tightening step. So reusing them is non-compliant. Decision must be fail. Risk: high or critical? Reusing torque-to-yield bolts can lead to joint failure, so critical. Work stage: in-progress (as given). Component conditions: non-compliant (since bolts are reused against spec). Percent complete: not specified, but we can estimate maybe 0? Actually, the work is in-progress, but the rework is happening, so maybe some progress. But we don't have a clear number. I'll put 0 as a placeholder, but it's not critical. Value: no numeric reading. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 1.0. Event time: not applicable. Rate: not applicable. Order: not applicable. Workable: not asked. Findings: "Reused single-use torque-to-yield bolts", "Non-compliant with engineering specification". Actions: "Stop work, replace bolts with new single-use bolts per spec", "Quarantine affected assembly". Rationale: Torque-to-yield bolts are designed for one-time use; reusing them risks insufficient clamping force and joint failure. The operator's reasoning prioritizes production speed over safety and compliance. References: engineering spec (single-use, angle-tightening). So JSON output.
